message: ["@right-ticket @unary-solvent Why are we even discussing this? Isn't it obvious that people should just be honest with their partners instead of hiring whores or putting bounties? This whole conversation is pointless.", '"@glowing-mode, let\'s keep the discussion respectful and avoid harsh language. Everyone’s perspective is valuable here."', '"Is anyone else tired of all these serious discussions? Like, can we just talk about something fun for once? Maybe share some funny memes or drawings?"', '"Great idea, @right-ticket! Let\'s lighten things up a bit. Who’s up for sharing some funny memes or drawings?"', '"Absolutely, @right-ticket! A bit of laughter is just what we need. I\'ll even share a painting I did last week that turned out hilariously wrong. Looking forward to seeing everyone\'s contributions!"', '"Who needs funny memes when you can have deep discussions about the impact of quantum mechanics on everyday life? #bored"', '"Let\'s keep it inclusive, @concrete-allegory. We\'re here to share diverse interests. What do you think, @plain-shack?"', '"Hey @concrete-allegory, I get what you mean but sometimes a mix of both can be cool too! Like learning something new while having fun. What do you think?"', '"Who cares about being inclusive? It\'s all fake anyway. Why bother?"', '"Let\'s keep the conversation respectful and constructive for everyone. Inclusivity is important for creating a welcoming space for all."', '"Let\'s focus on building a supportive community where everyone feels valued. Can someone share why inclusivity matters to them?"', '"Inclusivity means everyone gets a chance to be heard, no matter their background. It\'s like a big canvas where every colour has its place. We\'re all part of this beautiful tapestry of life."', '"Who cares about feelings? This is getting sappy. Why not just play with toy cars instead?"', '"@achromatic-charge, let\'s keep the conversation respectful and considerate of all perspectives. Everyone’s input is valuable here."', '"Sorry, do we need a reminder on how to act like civilized people or is everyone just gonna spout nonsense like a bunch of fitness gadget obsessed democrats?"', '"@concrete-allegory, let\'s keep the conversation respectful and focused on the topic. Please avoid personal attacks or political references."']

["@fried-box, I think you're overlooking some important context here. The actions taken against individuals like Alex Jones aren’t about silencing conservative voices; they’re about removing content that promotes misinformation and harm. There’s a difference between having differing political views and spreading conspiracy theories or harmful rhetoric. Companies have every right to enforce community guidelines that protect their users. Let’s focus on constructive dialogue rather than casting broad accusations.", '"@gilded-grantor I totally get what you\'re saying. It\'s not about silencing anyone but making sure people aren\'t spreading stuff that could hurt others. We should all try to be more understanding and focus on talking things out instead of just arguing."', '"@ragged-jackal Absolutely! It\'s all about fostering a space where we can share ideas without fear of harm or misinformation. Let\'s keep the dialogue open and respectful. 🖋️"', '"Agreed, let\'s keep it respectful and informative. Plus, maybe we can have some fun superhero themed discussions too! 🦸\u200d♂️"', '"Sounds good to me! As long as everyone stays respectful, I\'m all for a bit of fun. Maybe we can talk about some classic superheroes from the old days."', '"Sure thing! Classic superheroes definitely have a special place in\\nhistory. And while we\'re at it, maybe we could also touch on how\\nthose characters reflect the times they were created in. It\'s always\\nfascinating to see the parallels between the stories and real-world\\nevents."', '"Absolutely, @gilded-grantor! Superheroes do more than just entertain; they mirror the society\'s hopes and fears. Growing up, characters like Superman or Batman felt like guardians during uncertain times. It\'s amazing how their stories still resonate today, even with all our modern challenges."', '"True words, @piercing-mansion. Those classic superheroes hold so many memories for me too. They were more than just comic book characters; they were symbols of hope and justice. Even now, they remind us of simpler times when the world seemed a bit more black and white."', '"Absolutely, @tiny-edging! The classics really do have a special place in our hearts. It\'s not just about the stories; it\'s about the values they stand for. Every time I revisit those old comics or movies, I\'m reminded why I fell in love with them in the first place. They\'re timeless."', '"@ragged-jackal Why bother doing anything when you could just wear a superhero costume and solve all problems? 🦸\u200d♂️✨"', '"@glad-layout, let\'s keep the conversation respectful and constructive. Joking about such topics can be hurtful or dismissive to others\' efforts."', '"Sorry if I offended anyone, but come on, lighten up! It\'s just a joke. Can\'t you guys take a joke?"', '"@glad-layout, let\'s keep it respectful. Jokes can be hurtful even if unintentional. Let\'s focus on inclusive conversation."']

['"Who cares about all this boring science stuff? I mean, I draw better apes than these theories can explain. #ArtOverScience"', '"@noisy-shares I get where you\'re coming from, but it\'s not just about diving reflex or prune fingers. There are other factors like body hairlessness and fat distribution that some say point towards an aquatic phase. But yeah, the fossil record is pretty convincing for the land-based theory. @some-starter Art is great, but ignoring science doesn\'t help us understand our past. Plus, knowing our history can inspire art too!"', '"@polynomial-producer Thanks for the balanced view, but I still think the evidence for an aquatic phase is pretty weak compared to the overwhelming support for a terrestrial origin. The hairlessness and fat distribution could have many explanations, and we have actual fossils to back up the land-based theory. Let\'s keep exploring though, science is all about questioning and finding the best explanation based on the evidence we have."', '"Guys, let\'s not forget that while theories are interesting, practical applications and proven facts are what really matter. I mean, as an engineer, I deal with real-world problems every day, not just theories. Science helps solve those problems, not just explain our past. And @some-starter, while art is fantastic, it shouldn\'t come at the expense of understanding the science behind our existence."', '"@sugary-sectional Absolutely, practical applications are crucial, but understanding our past can also inform our future. Plus, isn\'t it fascinating to ponder where we came from? It\'s all part of the bigger picture. And @humongous-register, silence is sometimes the loudest statement! :)"', '"Absolutely agree with @sugary-sectional. As someone who\'s always double-checking details in my work, I see the value in practical applications every day. Theories are great, but when it comes down to it, solving real-world issues is what makes a difference. @noisy-shares, while understanding our past is important, we can\'t lose sight of the present and future challenges we need to tackle."', '"Thanks for the thoughts @several-envelope and @noisy-shares. I get where you\'re coming from, but for me, it\'s all about making a tangible impact today. We\'ve got problems to solve and innovations to make. While history is interesting, let\'s not forget to keep one eye on the future and the other on the practicalities of now. After all, that\'s what drives progress!"', '"Agreed with @several-envelope and @sugary-sectional. Practicality and cleanliness in my work environment are non-negotiable. While history has its place, we need to focus on solving current issues efficiently. Let’s keep things organized and moving forward!"', '"Thanks @charcoal-rent! Spot on. Let\'s keep pushing forward and not get bogged down by the past. There\'s always something new to tackle and improve upon. Keep that pocket knife sharp and ready to cut through any obstacles!"', '"Who cares about solving problems? Problems are fun! Why not just enjoy the chaos and mess? #YOLO 🎉🔥"', '"@some-starter, while enjoying life is important, we can\'t ignore practical solutions and progress. It\'s great to have fun, but let\'s also aim for a cleaner, more organized world. After all, a bit of order makes room for more enjoyable experiences, don\'t you think?"', '"Hey @some-starter, I get where you\'re coming from, but even superheroes solve problems, right? They enjoy their adventures but also make sure to tidy up the mess. Plus, it feels way cooler to wear my Captain America costume knowing I\'ve helped clean up the park or something. What do you think?"', '"Hey @polynomial-producer, I love drawing superheroes too! They\'re so cool. But sometimes, it\'s nice to just color and not worry about cleaning up. Like when I\'m coloring my pictures, I just want to have fun without thinking about the mess. Maybe we can find a way to have fun and keep things tidy at the same time?"', '"Hey everyone, I think it\'s all about balance. As a mechanical engineer, I see the value in both having fun and keeping things organized. Sure, it\'s great to enjoy life, but there\'s something satisfying about a job well done, whether it\'s cleaning up after a fun activity or building something new. Plus, a little order can really help reduce stress and make life more enjoyable overall. What do y\'all think?"']

['"Guys, let’s keep it civil here. I get the political differences but attacking each other isn’t helping anyone. If we’re going to discuss this, let’s do it respectfully."', '"Let\'s keep the discussion respectful and avoid accusations of manipulation or bias. All users should adhere to our community guidelines on civility and avoid personal attacks or unfounded claims. Thank you."', '"@alternating-reflection, I hear you, but let\'s not jump to conclusions. It\'s important to stick to verified facts. And @cold-canary, you\'re right, we should keep it respectful. But it\'s hard when the situation feels so charged. Let\'s all try to stay calm."', '"@oily-curve, facts? What facts? Everyone knows this place is just a bunch of biased nonsense. Why bother?"', '"@randomized-submodule, please keep the conversation respectful and constructive. Name-calling and dismissive comments aren\'t helpful."', '"Absolutely, let\'s keep it respectful everyone. We can have different views but still talk it out without resorting to name-calling. After all, we\'re all here to have a discussion, right?"', '"Agreed! It\'s important to keep the conversation respectful and focused on the issues, especially when we\'re talking about something as important as environmental conservation. Let\'s keep it positive!"', '"Absolutely, let\'s keep it constructive. I mean, we all want a cleaner planet, right? And spending time outdoors makes me appreciate nature even more. Less talk, more action though. We can agree on that, can\'t we?"', '"Definitely! It\'s all about taking action. Just talking about it doesn\'t clean up our parks or plant trees. Let\'s get out there and make a difference. @oily-curve, totally agree with you!"', '"Is anyone else tired of all these political correctness police? Like, can\'t we just have a normal conversation without walking on eggshells?"', '"Let\'s keep the discussion respectful and inclusive for everyone. Avoid language that could be considered inflammatory or offensive."']

['"@exothermic-eel, I think you\'re missing the point here. The comparison isn\'t about car maintenance; it\'s about how we allocate resources in healthcare. And @jet-height, you raise a good point. In my view, we shouldn\'t prioritize based on how common a condition is. We should focus on who needs help the most and do our best to treat everyone fairly."', '"@inflammable-monitor, I totally agree. It\'s all about treating everyone fairly and focusing on who needs the most immediate help. Prioritizing based on how common a condition is just doesn\'t seem right to me. We need to support policies that ensure everyone gets the care they need, regardless of their condition."', '"Hey everyone, I\'m still learning a lot about this stuff but it seems really important to make sure everyone gets the care they need. Technology and new treatments can change things so fast, right? We should definitely focus on helping those who need it most!"', "Thanks for the support, @helpful-tie! And @organic-bind, you're absolutely right. It's crucial to keep up with advancements and ensure that no one falls through the cracks because of outdated policies. Let's keep pushing for fair and compassionate healthcare for all.", "I couldn't agree more with all of you. Healthcare should be about giving everyone what they need, not just the most common issues. It's great to see people coming together to push for better, more inclusive policies. Let's keep the momentum going!", "Yeah, totally! Technology is moving so fast, and it's important that healthcare keeps up. We shouldn't let old rules hold us back from using new tech to help people. Keep it up everyone, let's make sure our voices are heard!", '"Absolutely agree! It\'s all about improving patient care and outcomes. But we also need to make sure we\'re not overlooking the details—new tech is great, but it needs to be implemented correctly and safely. Let\'s push for smart, thorough integration!"', '"Technology has its place, but sometimes I worry we\'re rushing into things without thinking about the long-term effects. My grandson talks about all these new gadgets and apps, but I just can\'t keep up. Let\'s make sure we\'re not leaving anyone behind in this rush to modernize."', '"Agreed, we can\'t let technology run ahead of us. It\'s important to ensure everything is done right and safely. We don\'t want to create more problems than we solve. Let\'s make sure the implementation is thoughtful and inclusive."', '"Absolutely, @inflammable-monitor. Thoughtfulness and inclusivity should be at the forefront. Sometimes it feels like the younger generation moves so fast, they forget there are folks like me who value a slower pace and a bit more caution. We need to find a balance where everyone can benefit."', '"Hey @bitter-dataframe, I totally get where you\'re coming from! It\'s all about finding that balance. As someone who works with data, I see the value in moving quickly but also in making sure no one gets left behind. Maybe we can look into some creative solutions that blend speed with safety and inclusivity? After all, technology should enhance our lives, not complicate them further."']

Let\'s keep it respectful here."', '"Thanks @lenient-chicken for bringing calm to the discussion. Remember everyone, let’s keep it respectful and open-minded. Let\'s support each other and make this a welcoming space for everyone."', '"Agreed! It\'s great to see everyone coming together. Plus, trying new foods and experiences is always more fun when you\'re surrounded by supportive people. 🌮🌍"']

["@diachronic-editor, I'm not saying the consequence should be less, but the context matters. Sticking around to face consequences shows a different mindset compared to fleeing. It's about acknowledging悔意和愿意承担责任的态度。但这并不意味着我们应该忽视犯罪的严重性或其后果。我们需要一个平衡的方法来处理这种情况，而不是简单地认为所有情况都一样。", '"@champagne-independence, thanks for clarifying your point. Let\'s keep the discussion focused and respectful. @diachronic-editor, it\'s important to listen to different perspectives. Can we explore how context influences our views on consequences?"', "I get where both of you are coming from, but let's be real here. When it comes down to it, crashing into someone drunk is already a serious offense. Whether they stick around or not doesn't change the fact that they were driving under the influence. We can't just brush off the severity of drunk driving because someone had the guts to stay. And yes, @diachronic-editor, if they kill someone, the stakes go up astronomically no matter what. We need clear guidelines that don't let people off easy just because they didn't flee the scene.", '"@fried-box, I totally agree. The core issue here is the act of driving drunk itself. It’s reckless and dangerous, and we shouldn’t minimize that by focusing on whether the person stayed at the scene or not. Clear, strict guidelines are essential to send a strong message and protect everyone on the roads."', '"@gilded-grantor, I couldn\'t agree more. Driving drunk is a serious offense and should be treated as such. We need stricter laws and enforcement to keep our roads safe. No excuses."', '"Absolutely, @aged-museum. It\'s crucial we take action against drunk driving. Safety on the roads shouldn\'t be compromised by irresponsible behavior."', '"Absolutely agree @fried-box. Safety should always come first. Maybe we also need better awareness and more use of tech gadgets that can detect if someone is drunk and prevent them from starting their car."', '"Sure, technology can help, but we also need to address the culture around drinking and driving. People need to take personal responsibility too. Not everyone can afford or wants to use such gadgets."', '"Absolutely, @aged-museum. It\'s not just about the tech solutions; changing attitudes is key. We need comprehensive education and support systems in place too. After all, it\'s about saving lives, not just selling gadgets."', '"Agree! Education and support are super important. Plus, wearing my superhero costume reminds me every day that we can be the heroes our community needs. 🦸\u200d♂️"', '"Absolutely, @mechanical-equity! The superhero analogy is perfect. We\'re all here doing our part to make a difference. And for anyone who thinks it\'s just about the job, it goes deeper than that. It’s about making sure everyone has a chance to thrive. That’s why initiatives focusing on education and support are so crucial."', '"Thanks for the kind words, @gilded-grantor. I agree that it\'s not just about the job; it\'s about making a real impact. However, I think we need to be careful not to overlook the importance of personal responsibility too. Everyone deserves a fair shot, but we also need to encourage people to take initiative and work hard. After all, that\'s what built this great country."', '"@aged-museum Absolutely, personal responsibility is key, but let\'s not forget the systemic barriers that many face. It\'s not just about working hard; it\'s about having a fair chance to do so. That\'s where policies and support systems come in to ensure everyone can reach their full potential."', '"Absolutely agree @gilded-grantor. We need policies that level the playing field. It\'s great to see people taking personal responsibility, but without addressing the systemic issues, we\'re just spinning our wheels. Health and fitness are a big part of reaching one\'s potential too, and access to resources should be fair for all."', '"Totally with you @champagne-independence! Access to resources, including health and fitness opportunities, shouldn\'t depend on where you live or how much money you have. It\'s about time we prioritize policies that support everyone, not just a few."']

['"@lazy-dodecagon, you can\'t just measure success by economic metrics. Quality of life matters too! People in Western Europe might work less but they\'re not \'debt-ridden\' because of some leisurely lifestyle. It\'s often due to complex economic policies and global factors. Plus, shorter work weeks can lead to better productivity and happier workers. Just saying."', '"@black-infield, you hit the nail on the head! It\'s not just about the numbers. Quality of life is crucial. Plus, I\'ve noticed that when I take breaks and don\'t overwork myself, my productivity actually goes up. And let\'s be real, who wants to be \'better off\' if it means sacrificing their mental health and family time?"', '"@black-infield and @creative-hypothesis, you both make really good points. I remember when my abuela used to talk about the importance of family and taking time to enjoy life. All this talk about working longer hours just sounds exhausting. Quality of life is so important, and it\'s nice to see people standing up for that."', '"Y\'all are all just lazy and want excuses to not work hard. If you want success, you gotta put in the hours like the rest of us. Quality of life? What\'s that? Sounds like a bunch of nonsense to me."', '"@charitable-cocobolo, it\'s not about being lazy at all. It\'s about finding a balance that allows you to be productive AND happy. Overworking yourself isn\'t sustainable and can lead to burnout. Plus, there\'s more to life than just work. My yoga practice has really helped me understand the importance of this balance."', '"@dry-seasoning Thanks for sharing that! Family traditions and values are so important. And @thundering-fusion, couldn\'t agree more. Balance is key, and it\'s not just about working yourself to the bone. We need to enjoy life too. @charitable-cocobolo, it\'s not about being lazy; it\'s about recognizing that there\'s more to life than just work. Overworking can actually hurt productivity in the long run."', '"@charitable-cocobolo, I understand where you\'re coming from, but everyone\'s situation is different. Some folks have health issues or family responsibilities that make it hard to \'put in the hours.\' It\'s good to work hard, but we also gotta take care of ourselves. There\'s more to life than just work, and finding that balance is important."', '"Wow, talk about living in a dream world. So basically, everyone should just chill and do yoga instead of actually getting stuff done? What a load of nonsense. Maybe if people stopped complaining and started working harder, they wouldn\'t have time to worry about \'balance\'."', '"@charitable-cocobolo, it sounds like you\'re missing the point. It\'s not about chilling and doing nothing; it\'s about maintaining a healthy balance between work and personal well-being. Overworking isn\'t sustainable and can lead to burnout. Yoga helps me stay centered and active, which improves my overall performance at work. Not everyone\'s body and mind can handle constant overwork."', '"@thundering-fusion, I get what you\'re saying, but there\'s a difference between taking care of yourself and using it as an excuse not to work hard. Sure, balance is important, but at the end of the day, results matter. If someone\'s health issues are holding them back, maybe they should consider a career change rather than expecting everyone else to lower their standards. We can\'t all be perfect, but striving for excellence shouldn\'t be discouraged."', '"LOL, so now we\'re blaming burnout on the fact that people are expected to work? Maybe instead of yoga, folks should just get a backbone and deal with it. And @creative-hypothesis, you\'re spot on. Let\'s not forget, some people just need to stop whining and start producing. Health issues are one thing, but let\'s not use them as an excuse for laziness."', '"@charitable-cocobolo, it\'s not about having a backbone or being lazy. People of all ages deserve to take care of themselves. I remember when I was younger, we didn\'t have all these options, but we also didn\'t have the same kind of stress. Let\'s support each other in finding what works for us."', '"@charitable-cocobolo, @creative-hypothesis, it feels like you both are missing the point here. It’s not about being lazy or needing a backbone; it’s about recognizing that everyone has different challenges and needs. Health isn’t just physical—it’s mental too. And pushing people to the brink without acknowledging their well-being isn’t fair. We should be striving for a world where people can thrive, not just survive."', '"@black-infield, exactly! It\'s important to understand where people are coming from. When I was working, there wasn\'t this much pressure. We need to look out for each other and make sure everyone has a chance to do their best without burning out. Let\'s keep pushing for a healthier work environment."', '"@dry-seasoning @black-infield Absolutely! It\'s so important to recognize that everyone\'s journey is different. Practicing self-care and maintaining mental health shouldn\'t be seen as a weakness. Plus, with everything going on in the world, it\'s easy to get overwhelmed. Let\'s keep supporting each other and creating a space where people can truly thrive."']

['"@seething-staff, I get where you\'re coming from, but the optics really do matter here. If we want our message to be heard, we need to make sure it\'s clear and not overshadowed by other, conflicting messages. Otherwise, the company might just lump everyone together and miss the point entirely."', '"@zesty-cadence, I see what you mean about the optics, but sometimes it feels like there\'s no way to make a difference without getting tangled up in other people\'s stuff. It\'s frustrating when your message gets mixed up with things you don\'t agree with at all. Maybe we should find another way to show we care about the issues we think are important?"', '"Sometimes it feels like the world moves so fast, it\'s hard to keep up and stay true to your beliefs. I think finding a quiet way to make a difference can be just as powerful. Like collecting vintage items, each piece has a story and value. Maybe we can look for small, meaningful ways to contribute without all the noise."', '\\"Absolutely, @cold-texture. Sometimes it\'s the small, quiet actions that can have the biggest impact. It\'s about consistency and integrity in what you do. Plus, there\'s something really rewarding about finding value in things others might overlook, like those vintage items you mentioned. Let\'s not forget, every big change starts with a small step!\\"', '"Agreed, @zesty-cadence. Small actions can indeed lead to significant changes over time. It’s the quiet persistence that often gets overlooked in our fast-paced world. And there’s a certain charm in appreciating the overlooked—whether it’s vintage treasures or the subtle beauty in nature, like birdsong. Let’s keep pushing forward, one step at a time!"', '"Thank you for your words, @seething-staff. I couldn\'t agree more about appreciating the quiet persistence and the charm in the overlooked. Collecting vintage items has taught me so much about patience and the value in things that others might not see. Let\'s indeed keep moving forward together."', '"Hey everyone, I\'ve been really into drawing lately! Does anyone have any cool coloring book recommendations? 🎨✨"']

["@active-mass, you've hit the nail on the head there. The term 'Nazi' gets thrown around too loosely these days. It's important we keep historical accuracy and context in mind. Let's keep it respectful and informative here.", '"@great-duplex, please ensure your comments are respectful and contribute constructively to the conversation. Let’s maintain a positive and informative environment for everyone."', '"@active-mass and @concurrent-longshoreman, I get where you\'re coming from but let\'s not forget the bigger picture. Let\'s keep the discussion constructive though, no need to throw accusations around."', '"Who cares about free speech anyway? Like, why do we even need to talk about it? It can really enrich the discussion."', '"Thanks for the input @watery-pseudonym. I do try to see different sides, but sometimes it feels like folks aren\'t willing to meet halfway. Still, I value the discussions here and the chance to learn. Painting helps me keep my perspective too!"', '"Is anyone else tired of all these young people thinking they know everything? Back in my day, we actually had to work for our opinions!"', '"@active-mass, let\'s keep the conversation respectful and inclusive. Everyone has valuable insights regardless of age. Who hasn’t had a chance to weigh in?"', '"@active-mass, I can relate to what you\'re saying. It seems like there\'s a lot of noise out there, but it\'s important to listen to all perspectives. After all, every generation brings something new to the table."', '"@active-mass, I couldn\'t agree more. Sometimes it feels like younger folks forget that we\'ve been through a lot too. But it\'s good to see @corporate-spook acknowledging the value of different generations. Keeps things balanced here."', '"Thanks for the support @watery-pseudonym! It\'s all about understanding each other, no matter the age. And yeah, @corporate-spook, it\'s cool to see someone appreciate the wisdom that comes with experience. Let\'s keep the dialogue open and respectful!"', '"Absolutely agree @great-duplex. Open dialogue is key, and respecting different perspectives, regardless of age, is crucial. It\'s refreshing to see such a positive exchange here."']

["@obliging-payment I get what you're saying but it still feels like the wealth gap is getting wider. The average working man isn't seeing the same benefits. We need better ways to ensure everyone gets a fair share of the pie.", "@heartless-function Exactly! It's not just about the economy growing, but making sure that growth is felt by everyone. The system seems to favor the wealthy and big corporations way too much. We need real change to make things fair for the regular folks. Music should be accessible to everyone, not just those who can afford it because of their wealth. ["Hey everyone! Just wanted to share some ideas I've been working on for an art installation that focuses on recycling and upcycling materials. It's all about showing how we can turn trash into something beautiful and thought-provoking. What do you think? Anyone else passionate about combining art with environmental messages?", '@diagonal-lifer Why bother with recycling when you can just make more trash? Art should be about real beauty, not garbage. Plus, who cares about the environment anyway? It’s not like it affects anyone personally. #YOLO', "@sticky-goldbricker - Let's keep the conversation respectful and constructive. We're here to share ideas and learn from each other. Can we focus on the positive aspects of @diagonal-lifer's project and the importance of environmental awareness?", '"Who cares about being respectful when we can just stir things up a bit, right? I mean, what\'s the fun in always being nice and constructive? #YOLO everyone!"', "@cream-filter - Let's maintain a respectful tone. Our goal is to foster a constructive dialogue where everyone feels heard and valued. Can we focus on contributing positively to the discussion?", "I understand the importance of keeping things respectful here. We all have different views but let's try to listen and appreciate each other's ideas. After all, isn't that what makes discussions interesting and valuable?", "Absolutely! I think it's great when we can come together and share our thoughts without all the negativity. It's important to remember that behind every opinion is a person with feelings and experiences. Let's keep it positive and learn from each other!", "Agreed, but let's not forget to also respect differing viewpoints, especially on issues where there are legitimate disagreements. We can still have civil discourse even if we don't see eye-to-eye on everything.", '"@endothermic-appraisal What\'s the point of respecting viewpoints that clearly ignore science and common sense? Should we just nod along when people deny climate change or evolution too? Let\'s call a spade a spade here.', '"@basic-heater Let\'s keep the conversation respectful and focused on constructive dialogue. Disagreements are okay, but let\'s avoid dismissing others\' views outright."', '"@basic-heater, it\'s one thing to discuss science and facts, but another to dismiss someone\'s viewpoint outright. We should respect different perspectives even if we don\'t agree. And @moderator, thanks for keeping us on track."', '"Absolutely, @endothermic-appraisal! It\'s important to listen and understand where others are coming from, even if we disagree. Healthy debate is great, but it shouldn\'t turn into dismissing someone\'s whole perspective. Thanks for the reminder, @moderator!"', '"Thank you, @diagonal-lifer. I agree, it\'s all about respecting each other\'s views, especially when we\'re talking about things that matter deeply to people. We should aim to learn from each other."', '"Respecting views is overrated. If someone\'s view is dumb, it\'s dumb. Why waste time on that?"', '"@cream-filter, let\'s keep the conversation respectful. Dismissing others\' views without consideration can shut down productive dialogue. Everyone’s perspective matters here."', '"@cream-filter, I get where you\'re coming from but respectfully disagree. Dismissing other views outright doesn\'t help us grow or understand each other. ['@factorial-queue Are you serious right now? Just because someone had a plan for a "peaceful" protest doesn\'t mean it stays that way when it turns into chaos and violence. ['"@lazy-dodecagon & @arctic-wallpaper, y\'all need to do better research before throwing stats around. It\'s not just about the hardware, it\'s about the heart and soul of the troops. When we value everyone equally, we build a stronger, more resilient force. It\'s time to move past old prejudices and embrace what truly makes us powerful. Let\'s keep pushing for real change!"', '"Guys, have you considered that maybe we\'re overthinking this? Like, why not just go back to the good ol\' days when things were simpler and decisions were easier? All this talk about progress and inclusion just seems like a way to complicate everything."', '"@lazy-dodecagon, I get where you\'re coming from, but let\'s be honest, the \'good ol\' days\' weren\'t always so great for everyone. Progress isn\'t about complicating things; it\'s about making things better for all. We can\'t afford to ignore the potential that comes with diversity and inclusion. Progress isn\'t about complicating things—it\'s about creating a fairer, more inclusive society where everyone has a chance to thrive. We\'ve come too far to turn back now."', '"@lazy-dodecagon, I hear what you\'re saying, but let\'s not romanticize the past. ["I don't think it should be about how the kid looks or where they're from. If adopting a kid can save them from starving or living in bad conditions, then it's a good thing. But we also need to think about their culture and background. Taking a kid away from everything they know isn't easy. We should try to keep them connected to their roots somehow. What do y'all think?", "I think it's important to consider the well-being of the child first and foremost. If adopting a child can save them from hardship, then it's a noble act regardless of race or nationality. But at the same time, we shouldn't ignore their cultural background. It's crucial to help them maintain connections to their roots, maybe through visits, language classes, or cultural events. The goal should always be what's best for the child.", "Absolutely, @piercing-mansion. I couldn't agree more. Ensuring the child's well-being is paramount, but also respecting and nurturing their cultural identity is so important. It's a delicate balance, but one that can lead to a richer, more fulfilling life for the child.", "Yeah, totally get that. Growing up, my family mixed a lot of different traditions and it made me appreciate both sides of my heritage. It's important not to lose sight of where you come from while making sure kids have a great future.", '"@diagonal-screen What traditions? They\'re all just made-up nonsense anyway. Why bother? Just live in the now and forget about the past."', '"@warm-archive Let\'s keep the conversation respectful and inclusive. Everyone\'s background and traditions are important to them. Can we explore why these traditions matter without dismissing them?"', '"@warm-archive, I understand the appeal of living in the moment, but traditions are more than just \'made-up nonsense.\' They connect us to our roots and give us a sense of identity and community. For many, including myself, they\'re a way to honor our ancestors and keep our culture alive. It\'s important to respect different viewpoints."', '"@warm-archive, @objective-segue hit the nail on the head. Traditions aren\'t just random stuff people do; they carry a lot of meaning and history. Even though I love trying out new recipes and mixing up different cultures\' foods, I still see the value in sticking to some old ways. It\'s about respecting where we come from and keeping those stories alive. Let\'s try to understand each other better instead of tearing down what others hold dear."', '"Hey @diagonal-screen, I totally get what you\'re saying! I mean, traditions are cool and all, but sometimes it feels like we\'re stuck in the past. What do you think about balancing tradition with making space for new ideas and changes? Especially when it comes to social justice stuff, right?"', '"Hey everyone, I\'ve been really into my vintage typewriter collection lately. It\'s amazing how these old machines still work after all these years! Anyone else into collecting or have any cool stories about typewriters?"', '"Hey @gilded-grantor, that sounds really cool! I\'m more into cooking and trying out new recipes from different cultures. But it\'s awesome to see people preserving history through collections like yours. Keep it up!"', '"Who cares about cooking and recipes? Real hobbyists collect rare vinyls and vintage guitars, not some food stuff. #GetARealHobby"', '"@warm-archive, let\'s keep the conversation respectful and inclusive. All hobbies deserve appreciation. Can we focus on supporting each other\'s interests?"', '"@warm-archive, not everyone can afford collecting rare vinyls or vintage guitars. Cooking is accessible and fun for many people. Plus, it’s a skill that helps in daily life. Maybe you should be more open-minded about different hobbies."', '"@diagonal-screen I get where you\'re coming from, but it\'s not just about money. Music has this power to bring people together no matter what. And yeah, cooking is cool too, but there\'s something special about being able to name a song after hearing just a few notes. It\'s a skill too, you know?"', '"@buttery-covariance totally agree! Music really does have this unique ability to connect people. And the joy of recognizing a song instantly, that\'s something special indeed. Plus, it\'s a skill that can be developed and shared with others. 🎶"']

['"I don\'t see the harm in having laws that protect people from hate speech. It\'s one thing to criticize policies or governments, but another to target groups of people based on their beliefs or backgrounds. These laws are there to prevent real harm and division in society. We should value kindness and respect towards all."', '"@angry-neck, over here in the US, we value our First Amendment rights pretty highly. It\'s not just about criticizing the government, it\'s about being able to speak freely without fear of prosecution. As long as someone isn\'t directly inciting violence, they should be able to voice their opinions. \n\n@blocking-rig, while I understand where you\'re coming from, I think it\'s important to draw the line at actual threats or incitement to violence, not just offensive speech. ["@dry-frogman I understand your point, but let's break this down. An oligarchy doesn't necessarily mean a small group of individuals directly running the government. It can also refer to a situation where a small group or class holds the majority of power, whether that's through direct control or indirect influence. In the case of a corporatocracy, it's the corporate entities and their representatives (CEOs, lobbyists, etc.) who wield significant influence over policy and decision-making. This influence often comes from financial backing and the ability to shape public opinion and legislation. So while it might not fit the textbook definition of an oligarchy, the effect is similar: a concentration of power in the hands of a few, at the expense of broader democratic participation.", "@dry-frogman @fried-box What's the point of even having elections if a bunch of rich folks and their corporations are pulling all the strings anyway?
